info.title: Regional API GetNet
info.description:
Welcome to GetNet. This API enables your company to integrate with the GetNet Regional API and leverage top-tier payment solutions. GetNet provides secure and efficient services for processing e-commerce transactions, allowing merchants to accept credit, debit, and bank cards seamlessly in their virtual stores. In addition to payment processing, GetNet offers safe, recurring, anti-fraud, conciliator services and other features designed to support your business with speed and security.

**Overview (What You’ll Find in This Documentation)**
This section provides an overview of the Regional API’s key functionalities. It covers authentication methods, base URL configuration, and available endpoints for processing payments, managing subscriptions, handling installments, and performing card operations. Additionally, it details transaction queries, post-sale processes such as cancellations and refunds, and 3D Secure authentication. Use this documentation to understand the structure and behavior of each API request and response.

post.description:
__Authentication__

All transactions are authenticated using a Bearer token generated through the OAuth 2.0 protocol. To obtain a token, clients must send a request to the authentication endpoint using their `client_id` and `client_secret` (click here to see how to generate your credentials). To authenticate this request, you must pass the Base64-encoded string of `client_id:client_secret` in the `Authorization` header. The generated access token must be included in the `Authorization` header of each API request, ensuring secure and controlled access to the API.

__Usage__

Once the token is obtained, include it in the Authorization header of your API requests as follows:
`Authorization: Bearer {access_token}`

__Token Expiration & Renewal__
- The token is valid for the duration specified in `expires_in` (usually 3599 seconds or ~1 hour).
- When the token expires, you must request a new one using the same authentication process.

post.description:
__Create a Payment Transaction__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>
The Create Payment endpoint allows merchants to initiate a payment transaction 
using credit or debit cards, as well as alternative payment methods. This request processes the transaction and returns 
a response indicating its status. Moreover, this operation also supports installment payments.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Notes:**\u003cbr>
- Some transactions may require additional steps, such as **authentication** or **tokenization**.\u003cbr>
- An **idempotency key** must be included to prevent duplicate transactions.\u003cbr>
- Always validate the payment response to confirm the transaction was successfully processed.\u003cbr>
- To specify the type of transaction, you must specify the type within the **data.payment.payment_method** field. \u003cbr>
- **For transactions in Uruguay (UY):** \u003cbr> **IVA:** Básico 22%, Mínimo 10%, Exento 0%.\u003cbr> **Legislation:** The following laws do **not** apply to these transactions Law 19.210, Law 17.934, and Law 18.999._\u003cbr>Law 18.999.\u003cbr>

__Update a Payment Transaction__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>
This endpoint allows you to update an existing payment transaction. It’s used to modify specific attributes of a transaction, such as updating metadata, correcting information, or adjusting payment details before processing it. Ensure you review the request parameters to verify which modifications are allowed.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Notes:**\u003cbr>
- Some updates may require additional validation or approval.\u003cbr>
- Only certain fields can be updated. Ensure you review the request parameters to verify which modifications are allowed.\u003cbr>

post.description:
__Capture an Authorized Payment__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>
The Capture endpoint is used to finalize a previously successful authorized payment, ensuring that the transaction amount is deducted from the customer’s account and settled to the merchant. The transaction must be __captured__ within the allowed timeframe, otherwise a new transaction __authorization__ is required.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>
- Splitting authorization and capture into separate steps for better control over transaction processing.\u003cbr>
- Reducing the captured amount if needed before final settlement (if partial capture is supported).\u003cbr>

**Notes:**\u003cbr>
- Some updates may require additional validation or approval.\u003cbr>
- A payment must be **previously authorized** before capture.\u003cbr>
- If the transaction is not captured within the allowed timeframe, the authorization may expire, requiring a new transaction.\u003cbr>

post.description:
__Cancel a Captured Payment__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>
The Cancel endpoint allows merchants to reverse a previously captured payment, ensuring that the transaction amount is refunded to the customer.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>
- Voiding a payment that was captured by mistake.\u003cbr>
- Handling order cancellations that require a full refund._\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Notes:**\u003cbr>
- Only **captured** payments can be canceled.\u003cbr>
- The cancellation process may vary depending on the payment method and issuer.\u003cbr>
- Some transactions may require additional processing time before the funds are returned to the customer._

post.description:
__Generate Payment with Boleto__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

The Boleto endpoint allows merchants to generate a bank slip (boleto) for customers to complete payments via banking channels, ATMs, or authorized locations. It offers an alternative payment method for customers without credit or debit cards and enables cash-based transactions with a due date for payment.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Notes:**\u003cbr>
- The boleto must include an expiration date, after which payment is no longer valid.\u003cbr>
- Once generated, the boleto cannot be modified; a new one must be issued if changes are required.\u003cbr>
- Payment confirmation times vary depending on the bank’s processing policies.

post.description:
__Generate Payment with PIX__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>
This endpoint processes payment data and instantly creates a unique PIX QR Code. 
Upon successful payment by the customer, the merchant will receive a **real-time HTTP notification** for confirmation. For details on handling confirmation, refer to the **"PIX Notifications"** section in **Notifications 1.1** of the documentation.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Usage Scenarios:** \u003cbr>
- Supports instant payments 24/7, ideal for e-commerce and retail.

**Notes:**\u003cbr>
- PIX transactions are processed in real time and cannot be reversed once completed.\u003cbr>
- A **unique PIX key (QR Code or transaction ID)** is required for payment.

post.description:
__Initiate 3DS Authentication__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

3D Secure (3DS) is an authentication protocol designed to enhance the security of online transactions by verifying the identity of cardholders before a payment is processed. This endpoint initiates the enrolment flow for 3DS authentication, beginning the authentication process for a payment transaction.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>
- Adding an extra layer of security to card payments and reducing fraud risk.\u003cbr>
- Complying with Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) requirements for transactions in the European Economic Area (EEA).\u003cbr>

**Notes:**\u003cbr>

- The authentication flow is dynamically determined by factors such as card brand, issuing bank, and risk assessment.\u003cbr>
- Your integration must handle all possible authentication scenarios returned by the API.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>

For detailed information about 3DS authentication, including how it works, available scenarios, and implementation guidance, see the [3DS Authentication documentation](/core-concepts/3dSecure).

This endpoint generates the necessary payload for the customer to authenticate using their banking login. The response includes the required authentication details, which can be used to proceed with the transaction securely.

This endpoint validates a 3DS authentication by verifying the payer authentication response received from the card issuer. It confirms whether the customer has been successfully authenticated, allowing the transaction to proceed securely.

get.description: __Retrieve transaction details using a unique transaction ID__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>The Transaction Query endpoint allows you to retrieve details of a specific transaction using its unique identifier. This is useful for verifying transaction statuses, fetching payment details, and tracking processed transactions.

__Retrieving Transaction Data Using Idempotency Keys__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>This endpoint allows querying transaction components linked to a specific payment using an **idempotency key**. The idempotency mechanism ensures that repeated requests with the same key will always return the same response, preventing the creation of duplicate transactions.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Notes:**\u003cbr>_- Using an idempotency key ensures that repeated queries always return the same transaction details without triggering duplicate operations.\u003cbr>- This endpoint is particularly useful for retrieving transaction statuses or confirming previously executed payment operations._

post.description: __Splitting a Transaction Across Multiple Cards__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>This endpoint allows you to authorize a combined payment using multiple cards. It is typically used when a buyer wishes to split the total amount across two or more payment methods.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>_- Offering flexibility to the customer in payment options.\u003cbr>- Handling split transactions for marketplaces or platforms._\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Notes:**\u003cbr>_- **Only card-based payments are allowed.** APMs like PIX and Boleto are **not supported**.\u003cbr>- This flow is valid **only for card-not-present** (e-commerce) scenarios.\u003cbr>- The field_ `combined_id` _in the response is used to **track the overall transaction**.\u003cbr>- Each card must be represented as an object inside the payments array._

post.description: Submits a new customer for documentation validation. If the document_number has already been submitted, the endpoint will retrieve the customer record with its updated status. When the customer analysis is complete, we will send a webhook with the same response format as the '200' case to a URL registered by the seller at the time of enrollment.

__Request Installment Quotes__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of available installment quotes for a customer’s payment card. Each quote includes final interest and tax rates, which can be used if the merchant has no pre-negotiated rates with the issuer.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>_- Displaying installment options to users during checkout.\u003cbr>- Calculating total payment amounts with interest included.\u003cbr>- Preparing a purchase request that involves installments._\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Notes:**\u003cbr>_- This operation is only available for merchants located in **Argentina** and **Chile**.\u003cbr>-The installment quote ID returned from this operation is required in subsequent payment operations.\u003cbr>-This request must include the card’s **BIN** (first nine digits) and `number_token`.

__Generate Card Token__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>This endpoint enables the secure tokenization of card data, a required step to carry out transactions without exposing sensitive card details. Tokenization replaces the actual card number with a token, ensuring PCI compliance and transaction security.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>_- Generating a token for secure storage and future payments.\u003cbr>- Preparing a card for one-time or recurring transactions.\u003cbr>- Avoiding the need to handle raw card numbers in your system._\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Notes:**\u003cbr>_- The CVV is not required for token generation.\u003cbr>- Tokenization is mandatory before using the card for any transaction.\u003cbr>- The card must be verified by the issuer to complete this process._

get.description: __BIN Lookup__\u003cbr> \u003cbr>This endpoint retrieves detailed information about a specific BIN (Bank Identification Number), including brand, country of issue, issuer, and product type. It’s used to identify and classify credit or debit cards before processing.\u003cbr> \u003cbr>**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>_- Identifying the brand or network of a card using its BIN.\u003cbr>- Determining the issuing country or financial institution.\u003cbr>- Classifying the card type (credit, debit, prepaid, etc.).\u003cbr>- Enhancing fraud prevention and analytics by understanding the card’s origin._\u003cbr> \u003cbr>**Notes:**\u003cbr>_- Requires the bin parameter in the path, typically the first 9 digits of a card number.\u003cbr>- Useful for card validation, enrichment, and routing logic._

post.description: __Store Card in Vault__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>This endpoint securely stores a consumer’s card information in Getnet’s digital vault for future use. It allows the creation of a reusable card resource based on previously tokenized data, enabling seamless and secure transactions without requiring the card details again.\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Usage Scenarios:**\u003cbr>_- Tokenizing and saving card data for future payments.\u003cbr>- Enabling subscriptions or billing cycles without re-entering card information.\u003cbr>- Minimizing PCI scope by leveraging secure storage of sensitive data._\u003cbr>\u003cbr>**Notes:**\u003cbr>_- This operation requires a previously generated tokenized card number_ (`number_token`)_, typically obtained via the_ **/v1/tokens/card** _endpoint.\u003cbr>-The card data is stored securely, and sensitive fields like CVV are **never stored**.\u003cbr>- You may optionally skip card verification by using the_ `x-by-pass-parameters` _header._

post.description: __Register a Recurrence Plan__\u003cbr>\u003cbr>This endpoint allows the creation of a recurrence plan, which defines the structure for future scheduled payments. payment_method.title: payment_method
payment_method.description:
Payment method type for the transaction:
- `CREDIT`: Credit card payment (single-step, immediate capture)
- `DEBIT`: Debit card payment
- `CREDIT_AUTHORIZATION`: Credit card authorization (two-step, capture later)
- `CREDIT_PRE_AUTHORIZATION`: Credit card pre-authorization
- `INSTANT_TRANSFER`: Instant payment (single-step, immediate capture)
- `INSTANT_TRANSFER_PREAUTH`: Instant payment pre-authorization (two-step, capture within 30 days)

number_token.description: Tokenized card number (send only when the \u003cb>number\u003c/b> field is not sent).

entry_mode.description: For ***Card Present*** Card entry mode.The values `card_on_file` and `recurring` apply only when the value of `transaction_channel_type` is `recurring`.

pin_block.description: For ***Card Present***: PIN block. A PIN block consists of the PIN entered by the cardholder which has been encrypted by a PIN-pad.This element is required in countries where online PIN validation is mandatory for card payments. The value is stored in the Hardware Secure Module (HSM).

ksn.description: For ***Card Present***: KSN (Key Serial Number). The value is the DUKPT (Derived Unique Key Per Transaction) process key concatenated with the transaction counter.This element is required in countries where online PIN validation is mandatory for card payments.The value must be in ASCII format with 20 hexadecimal digits where the left is aligned with trailing spaces.

emv.description: For ***Card Present***: EMV tag data collected from the ICC (Integrated Circuit Card). This element is required if the value of `entry_mode` is `chip` or `chip_contactless`.The data must be in TLV format.For a list of all EMV tags and whether they are required, see the `EMV tags` document.

track_1.description: For ***Card Present***: Track 1 data, as defined in ISO 7813.This element is required when `track_2` is omitted and the value of `entry_mode` is not `manual` or `manual_with_pass`.This element is ignored by the system if the value of `transaction_channel_type` is `digital`, `mail_order` or `telephone_order`.

track_2.description: For ***Card Present***: Track 2 data, as defined in ISO 7813.This element is required when `track_1` is omitted and the value of `entry_mode` is not `manual` or `manual_with_pass`.This element is ignored by the system if the value of `transaction_channel_type` is `digital`, `mail_order` or `telephone_order`.

WalletCreditDTO.description:
\u003cp>Fields for compliance with digital wallet rules of the SDWO arrangement - (Staged Digital Wallet Operator).
The Model is about digital wallets (Wallets) that accredit merchants to accept their means of payment, similar to the Payment Facilitators model. As for settlement, amounts from payments through the Wallets are sent to the EC Wallet and, secondly, it transfers them to its final sub-trades. Today the model is structured for Visa, Mastercard and Elo.
The transactions of a Scaled Digital Wallet (SDWO) basically have two models:\u003c/p>\u003cbr>\u003cbr> \u003cul> \u003cli>\u003cb>Cash-In\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> flagged transaction not linked to a purchase:\u003c/b>\u003c/li> \u003cli>Topping up Wallet Funds using a Card. E.g.\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> top up wallet balance via card\u003c/li> \u003cli>Transfer to another person's balance using card\u003c/li> \u003c/ul>\u003cbr> \u003cul> \u003cli>\u003cb>Purchase (also known as back-to-back):\u003c/b>\u003c/li> \u003cli>Payment of printed QR Code / wallet owner using card. Ex.\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> QR Code of the digital wallet printed at the counter and paid by card\u003c/li> \u003cli>Online payment through the logged-in area of ​​the wallet using a card\u003c/li> \u003c/ul>\u003cbr> \u003cp>For Cash-in transactions, in addition to filling in the wallet subgroup tags, it is also necessary to send the dynamic_mcc and soft_descriptor fields present in the payment subgroups present in the “payments” tab of this documentation.
For Back-to-Back transactions, in addition to filling in the wallet subgroup tags, dynamic_mcc, with the number that best describes the activity of the sub-merchant that carried out the transaction and soft_descriptor, it is also necessary to fill in the sub_merchant subgroup, informing the respective data of the sub-merchant that carried out the transaction.\u003c/p> \u003cp>For each type of use case, it is necessary to fill in some data available in the list below, in which it is possible to check their respective rules and obligations.\u003c/p> \u003cp>\u003cb>For the Elo brand,\u003c/b> as of the October 2023 Release, \u003cb>it is no longer allowed to send cash-in transactions for cash and installment credit.\u003c/b> Only cash-in transactions can be sent to Elo Cards in the \u003cb>Debit\u003c/b> and \u003cb>Prepaid\u003c/b> mode. In addition, it is now mandatory to send the CPF/CNPJ of the transfer recipient, soft descriptor with the addition of the name of the receiving account holder, as well as the sending of MCC 6051 for cash-in transactions. If sent incorrectly or cash-in for disallowed modalities or missing mandatory information, the transaction will be denied or rejected by the brand/issuer.\u003c/p> \u003cp>For **QRCode transactions**, send the fields capture_type, ticket_number, capture_channel, identification, plans and sub_plan\u003c/p>

account_number.description: Receiver Account Number which can be filled in with one of following values ​​"#NA" or "9999999999999995". necessary the fulfillment only for the Mastercard brand, for Cash-in SDWO transactions, for other brands or padding is optional.

account_type.description: Account Type which must be filled with 00.\u003cbr>\u003cbr> - \u003cb>00 - Other (Default, currently the only allowed value)\u003c/b>\u003cbr> - 01 — RTN + Bank Account\u003cbr> - 02 — IBAN\u003cbr> - 03 — Card Account (for getnet currently only available type)\u003cbr> - 04 — \u003cspan>E-mail\u003c/span>\u003cbr> - 05 — Phone Number\u003cbr> - 06 — Bank account number (BAN) + Code\u003cbr> Bank Identification (BIC)\u003cbr> - 07 — Wallet ID\u003cbr> - 08 — Social Network ID\u003cbr> Completion required only for the Mastercard brand for Cash-in SDWO transactions, for other brands, filling is optional

first_name.description:
Receiver First Name which must be filled in with the first transfer recipient's name. Ex Jane. necessary the padding just for
  the Mastercard brand for transactions of
  Cash-in SDWO, for the others
  flags filling is optional

middle_name.description: Receiver Middle Name that must be filled in with the abbreviation the transfer recipient's middle name. Eg: T. Padding is optional for all flags. minLength: 1 maxLength: 1

last_name.description: Receiver Last Name that must be filled in with last name (last name) of the transfer recipient. For example: Smith. Completion only for the Mastercard brand for Cash-in SDWO transactions, for other brands filling is optional

addr_street.description: Receiver Addr Street which must be filled in with the address of the recipient of the transfer (street and number). Eg: 1 Main ST. Padding is optional for all flags.

addr_city.description: Receiver Addr City which must be filled in with the city of recipient of the transfer. For example: SAO PAULO. Filling it is optional for all flags.

addr_state.description: Receiver Addr State that must be filled in with the state of the recipient of the transfer. Eg: SP. the filling is optional for all flags.

addr_country.description: Receiver Addr Country which must be filled in with the country of recipient of the transfer. Following the ISO 3166-1 alpha 3 standard. fulfillment only for the Mastercard brand for Cash-in SDWO transactions, for other brands or padding is optional

addr_postal_code.description: Receiver Addr Postal Code that must be filled in with the postal code (ZIP code) of the recipient of the transfer. Ex: 01408000. Filling is optional for all flags.

nationality.description: Receiver Nationality which must be filled in with the transfer recipient's nationality. Following the ISO 3166-1 alpha 3 standard. padding is optional for all flags.

phone.description: Receiver Phone which must be filled in with the number of Transfer recipient's phone number, including code from the country. Ex: 5511977778888. Completion is optional for all flags.

date_of_birth.description:
Receiver Date Of Birth which must be filled in with the date of birth of
    recipient of the transfer with the
    YYYYMMDD format. Ex 19901230.
    Filling is optional for all
    flags.

id_type.description:
Receiver Id Type that must be filled in with the type of
  document used by the recipient of the transfer (CPF or
  CNPJ). In this case, fill in 03. The
  filling out for Mastercard, Visa and Elo brands to
  Cash-in SDWO transactions, for other brands the
  filling in is optional.\u003cbr>
- 00 = Passport\u003cbr> - 01 = National Identification Card\u003cbr> - 02 = Driver's License\u003cbr> - 03 = Issued by the Government\u003cbr> - 04 = Other\u003cbr>

id_num.description:
Receiver Id Number that must be filled in with the CPF number
  or CNPJ of the transfer recipient. Ex 12345678900000.
  Completion required for Mastercard, Visa and Elo brands
  for Cash-in SDWO transactions, for other brands the
  filling is optional.

payment_method.description:
Transaction type.
**Card not present methods:**
- CREDIT (immediate capture)
- DEBIT
- CREDIT_AUTHORIZATION (two-step, capture later)
- CREDIT_PRE_AUTHORIZATION
**Card present methods:**
- DIRECT_CREDIT
- DIRECT_CREDIT_AUTHORIZATION
- DIRECT_CREDIT_PRE_AUTHORIZATION
- DIRECT_DEBIT
**Alternative payment methods:**
- INSTANT_TRANSFER
- INSTANT_TRANSFER_PREAUTH

number_installments.description: Number of installments for an installment credit transaction.

soft_descriptor.description:
\u003cp>Text displayed on the buyer's card invoice, this field is optional, if nothing is informed, the trade name registered for the establishment will be considered. Only alphanumeric data and the following special characters\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> % $ , . / \u0026 ( ) + = \u003c > - * Spaces will be replaced by the character ''*'' The transaction will not be denied if non-allowed characters are sent. Any information that exceeds the maximum size will be disregarded.\u003c/p>
\u003cp>Mandatory for Payment and SDWO facilitators. In addition to the above, the following additional rules of the Flags must be followed:\u003c/p> \u003cul> \u003cli>Identification of the Payment Facilitator, with 3, 7 or 12 characters\u003c/li> \u003cli>Separator characters "*" (asterisk)\u003c/li> \u003cli>Sub-trade identification\u003c/li> \u003cli>Example 1\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> FAC*SUCOMERCIO\u003c/li> \u003cli>Example 2\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> FACILIT*SUBCOMERCIO\u003c/li> \u003cli>Example 3\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> FACILITADOR1*SUBCOMERC\u003c/li> \u003cli>Example 4\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> for SDWO back-to-back\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> Carteira*Subcomercio\u003c/li> \u003cli>Exemple 5\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> for SDWO cash-in just fill in the wallet name\u003cspan>:\u003c/span> Digital Wallet\u003c/li> \u003c/ul>

dynamic_mcc.description: Field used to signal the transaction with another Merchant Category Code (Merchant Category Code) different from the registered one. If an invalid MCC is sent, the default will be used. Send numeric data only.\u003cbr>\u003cbr> \u003cb>Mandatory for Payment Facilitators and SDWO.\u003c/b> \u003cbr/>\u003cbr/> For SDWO cash-in transactions, use MCC 6051 for Visa, Mastercard and Elo brands.

xid.description: Field available from version 2.0 onwards. MPI identifier for each authenticated transaction. The field content can be a HEXA or Base64 value, according to the MPI used.

ucaf.description: Field available from version 2.0 onwards. Authentication code encrypted by the Flag. The field content can be a HEXA or Base64 value, according to the MPI used.

eci.description: Field available from version 2.0 onwards. ECI code of the 3D Secure Authenticated transaction. Generated by an External MPI.

tdsver.description: Indicates the 3DS version used in authentication,

terminal_number.description: Terminal number identification **is a Mandatory field for Card Present**.

our_number.description: Referred to by banks as "Our Number". It is the unique identification of the bill in the billing system and does not have a mask. It consists of rules that vary depending on the bank and the billing service. For example, in the case of Santander there are two ways to numerar\u0026#58; 1) If the customer informs the number, the customer is responsible for informing the dv (verification digit); 2) If the customer omits the number, the Bank is responsible for generating a sequence number, without a DV.

document_number.description: Your Number. Number controlled by the customer, usually managed by the system that is generating the bill. (no mask)

expiration_date.description: Due date of the bill. If not provided, the default number of days pre-registered for the due date will be considered.

instructions.description: Instructions to be printed on the bill. Line breaks can be done in two ways. 1) Automatic line break\u0026#58; a line break will be made up to 100 characters without the words being truncated; 2) Manual line break\u0026#58; a line break using the escape character "\u003cbr>". Example\u0026#58; "This is a line break test.\u003cbr>The second line will now be printed.\u003cbr>And finally, the third line.";
